claim: Birkhoff-ansatz characteristic equation for degree-(2,1) PCF y_{n+1} - b_n y_n + a_n y_{n-1} = 0 with b_n = b1 n + b0, a_n = a2 n^2 + a1 n + a0 is mu^2 - b1 mu + a2 = 0
Indicial closed form for the same family: alpha(mu) = -((b1-b0) mu + (a1-a2)) / (b1 mu - 2 a2), valid on the characteristic locus mu^2 = b1 mu - a2
Sum of indicial exponents (over both characteristic roots) equals (a1 - a2)/a2 = a1/a2 - 1; thus sum = 1 forces a1 = 2 a2.
Under sum=1 (a1 = 2 a2), product of indicial exponents = 2/9 forces  a2 = -9 b0^2 + 27 b0 b1 - 20 b1^2; equivalently, with r = b0/b1, a2/b1^2 = -9 r^2 + 27 r - 20.

The conjectured Trans-class ratio a2/b1^2 = -2/9 lies on the {1/3, 2/3}-indicial-locus only at the irrational points r = b0/b1 = (27 +/- sqrt(17))/18. Therefore no integer (a2, a1, a0, b0, b1) PCF can simultaneously have indicial pair {1/3, 2/3} AND a2/b1^2 = -2/9.
Compatibility ideal C for indicial pair {1/3, 2/3} is C = (a1 - 2 a2,  a2 + 9 b0^2 - 27 b0 b1 + 20 b1^2); a0 enters only at the [n^{-1}] balance and is unconstrained at this order.

Numerical least-squares fit at mpmath dps=150 over n in [200,600] of  ln|P_n| - ln Gamma(n+1) - n ln|mu_dom|  vs  ln n  agrees with the symbolic dominant alpha to ~10^{-3} on five test families (T1,T2,NT1,NT2,LOG1).

For two integer Trans-style families (a2/b1^2 = -2/9, b1=3, a2=-2): family T1 (a1=-4, b0=0) has indicial exponents { -0.5914..., 1.5914... }; family T2 (a1=0, b0=1) has exponents { -0.6213..., -0.3787... }. Neither equals {1/3, 2/3}.

Integer-grid search over |b1| in [1,30], |b0| <= 30, |a2| <= 200 finds 0 simultaneous solutions of (E) 9 a2 + 2 b1^2 = 0 and (C) C1=C2=0 (610 E-only and 205 C-only). This empirically confirms no integer Trans family lies on the {1/3, 2/3} indicial locus.

hypothesis: indicial pair {1/3,2/3} forces a2/b1^2 = -2/9 via compatibility
indicial_equation: alpha(mu) = -((b1-b0) mu + a1 - a2)/(b1 mu - 2 a2),  with mu^2 = b1 mu - a2
numerical_verification: 5 families fitted at dps=150; symbolic alpha(mu) verified to ~10^{-3} on the dominant exponent; Trans-style families T1,T2 do NOT exhibit indicial pair {1/3,2/3}
